item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The third image in this article is from Wikimedia Commons and is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). The first two images can be found here and here which are non-compliant. Thus, they were removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't understand why the dates, and the dispersal method for SCP-3778-B, are censored. Wouldn't that info be necessary for personal working on the Methuselah Project?

Aside from that, this doesn't do anything to differentiate itself from other "animal society" SCPs, and I felt the interview was overly-cutesy without any real meat to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thanks for the heads up. I'm still sorta new to the site, so thank you for letting me know the protocol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Echoing the above commentators, this doesn't really feel in line with the Foundation. Cooperation, especially using anomalies or memetics to promote an anomaly's uncontained status, is not usually in the cards.

I also am still not a fan of "Threat Level" as it adds nothing narratively, beyond trying to tell us how super dangerous something is. We can already get that from the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please do not tag your own articles if you are unfamiliar with our tagging process. I have cleared the tags, but in the future, please wait until a member of the tag team can add them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just another "animals forming societies" skip. It's nothing new for me, it's been done three times, and I've grown tired of it. Sorry, but -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this up until the point where I questioned why the Foundation is putting so much effort into playing nice with these guys. Don't have to destroy them, obviously, but why aren't we trying to keep their population down, find ways to counteract or limit the spread of the grapes, and just generally lock these things up? They're just smart opossums.

The rapid technological advancement of SCP-3778-A may potentially pose an SK-Class dominance shift.

Being rather redundant with the sentence before it, you could easily replace everything before "may" with a comma and 'which'. On that note, don't capitalize the second word in a scientific name. I also take umbrage with the way the opossum's text is written in the interview; if you're going to go phonetic to show that they talk weird, don't use odd symbols that don't necessarily have a specific sound associated with them. Novote, but this certainly is not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SK-Class Dominance Shift is one of the relatively few K-classes that had a more-or-less agreed upon definition prior to the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! I understand that the Foundation has a contain policy, which is exactly why they are trying to manage SCP-3778-A, not plain exterminate them. SCP-3778 has to be destroyed because it is very difficult to tell from an ordinary grapevine, and because of the potential risks it may cause if it escapes Foundation control.

Nonetheless, I'm really glad that you enjoyed this skip. I hadn't thought of putting this forward into doomcon, but maybe I'll join a team with 3778, now that you mention it.

Your feedback is appreciated <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the idea of this SCP, but you're missing one big thing in regards to the Foundation's mindset: contain, not destroy. I'd say the Foundation would go as far as doing just about anything to contain an anomaly, while the GOC is more focused on the "destroy" part. I just think that focusing more on "contain" would be much more interesting for something like this rather than "all instances are to be destroyed."

Nonetheless, upvoted. An "SK-Class Dominance-Shift Scenario" really grabbed my interest. Not sure if this is a Doomcon entry, but, this part still interested me very much.

e: On second thought, you do say "uncontained" is to be destroyed. I feel that it should be something like "if an instance is uncontained, the UIU or GOC are allowed to destroy said instance" instead of the destruction aspect being the Foundation's job.
